description: Rebrand and productize react-graph as an open-source CLI (rgk) that makes local infra, indexing, and MCP workflows easy for React/Next.js repos, including messy codebases.

userStories.title: Finalize package identity and config schema
userStories.description: Update package.json with new identity (@rgk/react-knowledge-graph), set bin field to rgk, define RGK_ env vars, create rgk.config.json schema, and establish TypeScript config interface.
acceptanceCriteria: package.json name is @rgk/react-knowledge-graph
package.json bin field maps rgk to dist/cli.js
src/types/config.ts exports RgkConfig interface covering neo4j, indexing, and classification options
Default config file name is rgk.config.json
Env var names: RGK_CONFIG, RGK_NEO4J_URI, RGK_NEO4J_USER, RGK_NEO4J_PASSWORD, RGK_NEO4J_DATABASE
TypeScript compiles without errors

userStories.title: Create CLI entrypoint and command router
userStories.description: Create src/cli.ts that uses util.parseArgs to parse arguments and dispatch to subcommands (infra, index, mcp, help, version). Unknown commands exit 2, --help works on root and subcommands, --version prints package version.
acceptanceCriteria: src/cli.ts exists and is the bin entrypoint
Routes to subcommands: infra, index, mcp, help, version
Unknown command prints usage to stderr and exits with code 2
--help flag works on root command and each subcommand
--version prints package version from package.json and exits 0
Unit tests cover: unknown command exits 2, --help exits 0, --version exits 0
TypeScript compiles without errors
All tests pass

userStories.title: Implement config loader with deprecation compatibility
userStories.description: Refactor src/config.ts to check RGK_ env vars first, fall back to old REACT_GRAPH_*/NEO4J_* vars with deprecation warnings. Load rgk.config.json by default, fall back to graph-config.json. New names take precedence when both exist.
acceptanceCriteria: src/config.ts exports loadConfig() that returns RgkConfig
Precedence: CLI flags > RGK_ env vars > REACT_GRAPH_/NEO4J_ env vars > config file > defaults
rgk.config.json is primary config file, graph-config.json is fallback
When old names are used, deprecation warning printed to stderr with replacement guidance
When both old and new names exist, new names take precedence
Unit tests cover: new env vars, old env vars with warning, both present, config file fallback
TypeScript compiles without errors
All tests pass

userStories.title: Extract index service from existing code
userStories.description: Extract indexing logic into src/core/index-service.ts with a runIndex(options) function. Flow: parse workspace with ts-morph, clear graph, ingest nodes/edges, return stats. Both CLI and MCP server should use this service.
acceptanceCriteria: src/core/index-service.ts exports runIndex(options) function
Options include: workspacePath, configPath, outputFormat
Flow: parse workspace -> clear graph -> ingest nodes/edges -> return stats
Returns structured result: { nodeCount, edgeCount, classificationSummary }
Existing MCP index tool calls runIndex instead of inline logic
Unit test with fixture directory yields expected node/edge counts
TypeScript compiles without errors
All tests pass

userStories.title: Extract health service for diagnostics
userStories.description: Create src/core/health-service.ts with diagnostic functions for Neo4j connectivity, auth validity, config file presence, and Docker status. Returns structured health report used by mcp doctor and infra status.
acceptanceCriteria: src/core/health-service.ts exports runDiagnostics() function
Checks: Neo4j connectivity, Neo4j auth, config file exists, Docker running
Returns structured report: array of { check, status: pass|fail, message }
Unit tests cover: all-healthy scenario, neo4j-down scenario, missing-config scenario
TypeScript compiles without errors
All tests pass

userStories.title: Implement rgk infra up/down/status commands
userStories.description: Implement infra subcommands that manage Neo4j via docker compose. 'up' starts services with optional --wait polling, 'down' stops with optional --volumes, 'status' reports state using health service.
acceptanceCriteria: rgk infra up starts Neo4j via docker compose up -d
rgk infra up --wait polls Neo4j health until ready (default 60s)
rgk infra up --wait --timeout 120 overrides wait duration
rgk infra down stops services; --volumes also removes data volumes
rgk infra status reports running/stopped state and Neo4j health
Exit code 0 on success, 1 on failure
Integration test: infra up --wait reaches healthy Neo4j, infra status reports healthy, infra down cleans up
TypeScript compiles without errors
All tests pass

userStories.title: Implement rgk index CLI command
userStories.description: Wire up the index subcommand to call index-service. Supports workspace path argument, --config, --stats, --json flags. Human-readable output by default.
acceptanceCriteria: rgk index [workspacePath] indexes the given or current directory
--config <path> overrides config file location
--stats prints summary (nodes, edges, classifications) to stdout
--json outputs machine-readable JSON
Default output is human-readable with colors
Exit code 0 on success, 1 on error
Integration test: index fixture directory yields expected counts
Integration test: --json output matches stable schema
TypeScript compiles without errors
All tests pass

userStories.title: Implement adaptive classification for messy repos
userStories.description: Add classification layer to indexer that infers file kind and domain tags using AST + path signals, with confidence scores. Report coverage by confidence bucket and detect hotspots. Support config override rules.
acceptanceCriteria: Infers kind: next_page, next_layout, next_route, react_component, hook, util, unknown
Infers domain tags from paths/imports: admin, profile, storefront, api, etc.
Each inferred label has confidence score 0-1
Coverage report: breakdown by confidence bucket (high >0.8, medium 0.5-0.8, low <0.5, unknown)
Hotspot detection: high fan-in, high fan-out, orphans, duplicate/similar candidates
Config supports path/glob rules to override kind/domain/layer
Messy fixture repo indexes successfully with partial unknown (no hard failure)
TypeScript compiles without errors
All tests pass

userStories.title: Implement rgk mcp start and rgk mcp doctor
userStories.description: Extract MCP server startup from src/index.ts into src/mcp/server.ts. Wire up mcp start (stdio foreground) and mcp doctor (health checks) subcommands. Update server identity to rgk.
acceptanceCriteria: src/mcp/server.ts exports startMcpServer() extracted from src/index.ts
rgk mcp start launches MCP server over stdio in foreground
rgk mcp start --config <path> overrides config
MCP server identity is rgk (not react-graph-mcp)
All existing MCP tools remain functional
rgk mcp doctor runs health checks and prints diagnostic report
src/index.ts remains as compatibility wrapper calling src/mcp/server.ts
Exit code 0 on success, 1 on failure
TypeScript compiles without errors
All tests pass

userStories.title: Rebrand all user-facing output
userStories.description: Ensure all logs, help text, error messages, and server identity use RGK branding. No stale react-graph references in user-facing output.
acceptanceCriteria: All CLI help text references rgk command and RGK product name
All error messages use RGK branding
MCP server name is rgk in protocol responses
grep -r 'react-graph' on src/ returns zero matches in user-facing strings (internal code refs OK)
Rebrand regression test: scan all stdout/stderr output for stale naming
TypeScript compiles without errors
All tests pass

userStories.title: Write documentation (README, CLI reference, migration guide)
userStories.description: Create README.md with 30-second quickstart, CLI.md with command reference, and MIGRATION.md with old-to-new mapping table and deprecation timeline.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md has 30-second quickstart with npx and global install examples
README.md has first-run troubleshooting section
CLI.md has command-by-command reference with examples for all subcommands
MIGRATION.md has old->new mapping table for env vars, config files, and CLI commands
MIGRATION.md states deprecation timeline and removal target version
All code examples in docs are accurate and match current implementation

userStories.title: Set up CI pipeline and npm release automation
userStories.description: Create GitHub Actions CI that triggers on vX.Y.Z tags. Pipeline: install, build, typecheck, test (unit + integration), publish to npm, create GitHub release.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/release.yml exists
CI triggers on tags matching vX.Y.Z pattern
Pipeline runs: npm install, npm run build, tsc --noEmit, npm test
Integration tests run with Docker (docker compose for Neo4j)
On success: publishes to npm and creates GitHub release
rgk version output matches the tag version
TypeScript compiles without errors
